rounds.title: Implement a sparse matrix iterative solver toolkit in C called `nlsolve`. The tool reads a
rounds.md:
Implement a sparse matrix iterative solver toolkit in C called `nlsolve`. The tool reads a linear system Ax = b from files and solves it using iterative methods, outputting the solution vector and convergence statistics. It also supports matrix analysis and bandwidth-reducing reordering. ## Matrix Format Use the Matrix Market coordinate format (.mtx). The format is: ``` %%MatrixMarket matrix coordinate real symmetric % optional comment lines ROWS COLS NNZ row col value row col value ... ``` For symmetric matrices, only the lower triangle (including diagonal) is stored. The tool must expand symmetric storage internally. Row and column indices are 1-based in the file. Also support `general` symmetry (all entries explicitly listed). ## Vector Format Plain text, one value per line: ``` N value1 value2 ... ``` The first line is the dimension, followed by N floating-point values. ## Solvers Support two iterative solvers: 1. **Conjugate Gradient (CG)** - for symmetric positive definite (SPD) systems, with residual-based convergence: stop when `||r_k||_2 / ||b||_2 < tol`. 2. **BiCGSTAB** - for general non-symmetric systems, with the same relative residual convergence criterion. ## Preconditioners Implement three preconditioners: 1. **Jacobi** - diagonal scaling: M = diag(A) 2. **SSOR** - Symmetric Successive Over-Relaxation with omega=1.2. 3. **IC(0)** - Incomplete Cholesky preconditioning with zero fill-in, using the lower-triangular sparsity pattern of A. Only valid for SPD matrices used with CG. ## Matrix Reordering When `--reorder rcm` is specified, apply Reverse Cuthill-McKee (RCM) reordering before solving. The matrix graph should be ordered deterministically by node degree and original node index, then rows and columns of A and entries of b should be permuted consistently. The reported solution must always be converted back to the original variable ordering before output. ## Matrix Analysis When `--analyze` is specified instead of `--solver`, print analysis to stdout: ``` dimension: nonzeros: symmetric: bandwidth: profile:

diag_dominant: ``` Where: - **bandwidth** B = max over all non-zero a_{ij} of |i - j| - **profile** P = sum over all rows i of (i - min column index in row i). Only count rows that have at least one off-diagonal entry; rows with only a diagonal entry contribute 0. - **diag_dominant**: "strict" if |a_{ii}| > sum of |a_{ij}| for all j != i, for every row; "weak" if |a_{ii}| >= sum for every row (but not all strict); "no" otherwise. ## Matrix Generator Include a built-in 2D Laplacian matrix generator. For an n*n grid, produce the standard 5-point stencil matrix of dimension n^2 * n^2. The diagonal is 4.0, and off-diagonals are -1.0 for adjacent grid points (both within-row and between-row connections). Write the generated matrix as Matrix Market `real symmetric` coordinate data using lower-triangle storage. Also include a 3D Laplacian generator for an n*n*n grid, producing the standard 7-point stencil matrix of dimension n^3 * n^3. The diagonal is 6.0, and off-diagonals are -1.0 for the 6 neighbors (x+1, x-1, y+1, y-1, z+1, z-1). Write the generated matrix as Matrix Market `real symmetric` coordinate data using lower-triangle storage. ## Command-Line Interface ``` nlsolve --input --rhs --solver \ --precond --tol --maxiter \ [--reorder rcm] [--history] nlsolve --input --analyze nlsolve --generate-laplacian --output nlsolve --generate-laplacian3d --output nlsolve --generate-rhs --output ``` The `--generate-rhs` command produces a vector of dimension N where the i-th value (for i = 1, 2, ..., N) is sin(i * pi / (N+1)). The `--history` flag causes the solver to print one line per iteration before the solution output: `iter : residual = ` using `%.6e` format for the residual. Iteration numbering starts at 1. ## Output Format For solve mode, print to stdout: ``` solver: preconditioner: dimension: reordering: iterations: residual_norm: converged: solution: x[0] = x[1] = ... x[N-1] = ``` All floating-point values in the output use `%.6e` format (6 decimal places, scientific notation). For generate modes, write the file and print nothing to stdout. ## Build The project must compile with `make` producing the `nlsolve` binary. A simple Makefile with `gcc -O2 -lm` is sufficient. ## Defaults - `--tol` defaults to `1e-6` - `--maxiter` defaults to `10000` - `--precond` defaults to `none` - `--reorder` defaults to `none` (no reordering)

rounds.title: Error handling is non-existent — bad files cause segfaults, unknown options are silently i
rounds.md: Error handling is non-existent — bad files cause segfaults, unknown options are silently ignored, and dimension mismatches produce garbage. Every invalid-input path must print a single line `error: <description>` to stderr and exit with code 1. This covers: missing or unreadable files, malformed Matrix Market headers, non-numeric data in matrix or vector entries, NNZ count mismatches in the data section, extra trailing data beyond the declared entry count in matrix or vector files, matrix-RHS dimension mismatches, non-square matrices for solver mode, unknown solver / preconditioner / reordering names, numerical breakdowns (zero or negative pivot) during IC(0) factorization, conflicting or incomplete command-line arguments (e.g. `--solver` without `--input`, or specifying more than one operation mode at once such as `--analyze` with `--solver`, or combining any two generator flags like `--generate-laplacian` with `--generate-rhs`), and invalid sizes for generators (zero or negative values for `--generate-laplacian`, `--generate-laplacian3d`, or `--generate-rhs`). The CG solver must also detect non-positive-definite matrices at runtime: if the denominator p^T A p becomes zero or negative during any iteration, immediately print `error: matrix is not positive definite` to stderr and exit with code 1. The BiCGSTAB solver must detect numerical breakdowns at runtime: if the scalar rho (r0^T r) becomes zero or the denominator r0^T v becomes zero during any iteration, immediately print `error: BiCGSTAB numerical breakdown` to stderr and exit with code 1. Stdout must be empty on any error exit. All output for valid inputs stays exactly the same.

rounds.title: Code review found correctness bugs that silently produce wrong numerical results on valid
rounds.md:
Code review found correctness bugs that silently produce wrong numerical results on valid inputs. Fix every issue listed below. All output for inputs that do not trigger these bugs must remain exactly the same.

1. The `residual_norm` field uses the iteratively-updated residual, which accumulates floating-point rounding errors over many iterations and can diverge from the actual error. After the solver loop finishes, explicitly recompute the true residual as `||b - A * x||_2 / ||b||_2` using the computed solution vector and report that value as `residual_norm`. The `converged` field must also be based on the true residual (yes if and only if the true residual is below tolerance). The per-iteration `--history` lines should continue using the iterative residual. The `iterations` count remains whatever the solver loop produces.

2. The Matrix Market reader does not handle duplicate entries. The format allows multiple entries for the same (row, column) position; their values must be summed to produce the correct matrix element. This applies to both raw file entries and entries generated by symmetric expansion: if a symmetric file lists the same lower-triangle position twice, symmetric expansion produces four COO entries for those two positions, all of which must be merged. Currently, each occurrence becomes a separate CSR entry, silently corrupting the matrix.

3. When every entry of the RHS vector is zero, the current `if (bnorm == 0) bnorm = 1` workaround inside the solvers masks the edge case. With the true-residual post-computation from fix 1, this would compute 0/0. Instead, detect `||b||_2 == 0` before calling the solver and immediately output the zero solution: `iterations: 0`, `residual_norm: 0.000000e+00`, `converged: yes`, and every `x[i] = 0.000000e+00`. This applies to both CG and BiCGSTAB.

4. The SSOR preconditioner divides by diagonal entries of A without checking for zero. If any diagonal entry is exactly zero, the division produces NaN/Inf that propagates through the entire solve. In both the forward and backward sweeps of SSOR, if the diagonal entry for a row is zero, set the corresponding output component to zero instead of dividing.

rounds.title: Code review found a correctness bug in RCM reordering and identified a missing solver that
rounds.md:
Code review found a correctness bug in RCM reordering and identified a missing solver that should be available.

1. **RCM reordering is broken for disconnected matrices.** On matrices whose graph has multiple connected components, the current reordering only reduces bandwidth for the first component and leaves later components effectively in their original order. RCM should handle disconnected graphs consistently:

   - Handle each connected component independently.
   - Within each component, choose the same kind of pseudo-peripheral start and degree-ordered traversal used for connected matrices.
   - Reverse each component's Cuthill-McKee ordering independently before concatenating into the final permutation.
   - Components are ordered by their smallest node index (the component containing the lowest-index node comes first in the permutation).

2. **Add GMRES(m) restarted solver.** Implement the restarted Generalized Minimal Residual method as `--solver gmres`. Add a `--restart <m>` parameter (default 30) controlling the Krylov subspace dimension before restart.

   - On restart, use the current approximate solution as the new initial guess and recompute the residual.
   - Convergence check: after each Krylov step, check the current relative residual estimate against `tol`; when it is below tolerance, return the updated solution.
   - `--history` prints one line per inner iteration across all restart cycles, with globally sequential iteration numbering starting at 1.
   - The `iterations` count in the output is the total number of Krylov steps across all restarts.
   - True residual reporting and the `converged` field work identically to CG and BiCGSTAB (true residual recomputed after the solver returns).
   - All existing preconditioners (none, jacobi, ssor, ic0) must be accepted and applied for GMRES.
   - If `--restart` value is less than 1, print `error: invalid restart value` to stderr and exit non-zero with no stdout.
   - If `--restart` is given without `--solver gmres`, print `error: --restart requires --solver gmres` to stderr and exit non-zero with no stdout.

All existing output for CG, BiCGSTAB, analyze, and generator modes must remain exactly the same.

rounds.title: Code review determined that aborting the process on numerical errors was the wrong design
rounds.md:
Code review determined that aborting the process on numerical errors was the wrong design choice. Numerical issues during solving (non-positive-definite detection, BiCGSTAB breakdown) should produce a partial result rather than terminating with only an error message. Input validation errors remain unchanged.

1. **CG non-positive-definite detection must produce partial output instead of exiting.** When CG detects `pAp <= 0`, it must stop iterating and output the current partial solution with the standard fields: `solver`, `preconditioner`, `dimension`, `reordering`, `iterations` (the count so far), `residual_norm` (true residual of the partial solution), `converged: no`, and all `x[i]` values. The `--history` output up to that point is unchanged. Stderr must be empty. Exit code must be 0.

2. **BiCGSTAB breakdown must produce partial output instead of exiting.** When BiCGSTAB detects a breakdown (rho near zero or denominator near zero), it must stop iterating and output the current partial solution with the same standard fields. `converged: no`, and all `x[i]` values of the partial solution. Stderr must be empty. Exit code must be 0.

3. **IC(0) non-positive pivot must fall back instead of exiting.** When IC(0) factorization encounters a non-positive diagonal during factorization, instead of aborting, fall back to no preconditioner: discard the incomplete factor, run the solver with `preconditioner: none`, and report `preconditioner: none` in the output. This applies whenever `--precond ic0` is requested, including CG, BiCGSTAB, GMRES, and runs that also use reordering. Stderr must be empty. Exit code must be 0.

All input/CLI validation errors (missing files, malformed data, unknown options, conflicting flags, dimension mismatches, invalid sizes) continue to exit non-zero with `error:` on stderr and empty stdout, exactly as before.
